name: Creu d'Aragall and Santa Creu d'Olorda Loop
name_original: Santa Creu d’Olorda - circular por el Parque Natural de Collserola y el Llobregat
description: This difficult 44.5-mile road cycling route through Collserola Natural Park offers challenging climbs and panoramic views of Barcelona.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Barcelones feature a varied landscape, encompassing coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea, extensive natural parks like Collserola, and challenging ascents into surrounding hills. The region is characterized by its blend of urban areas, river valleys, and elevated terrain, providing diverse cycling experiences. This geographical mix allows for routes ranging from flat seaside trails to demanding climbs with significant elevation gain. Best road cycling routes around Barcelones The most popular road cycling route is Paseo marítimo de Premia de Mar y río Besós - circular route of Barcelona north, a 40.0 miles (64.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows coastal and river paths north of the city. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From the Turó Viewpoint to Tibidabo — Collserola Natural Park, a moderate 24.4 miles (39.2 km) path. This route includes climbs within Collserola Natural Park, leading to the Tibidabo viewpoint. Local road cyclists also love the Collserola Park and Rabassada Climb Loop, a 48.3 miles (77.7 km) trail leading through Collserola Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 23 minutes. Road cycling around Barcelones is defined by coastal paths, natural park trails, and demanding hill ascents. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy seaside rides to difficult mountain challenges. The routes in Barcelones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 40,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Barcelones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Barcelones?
A: There are over 120 road cycling routes in the Barcelones region, offering a wide range of experiences for all skill levels. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Barcelones?
A: The mild climate in Barcelones makes it suitable for road cycling almost year-round. However, spring and autumn are generally considered optimal due to pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather conditions. Even in winter, coastal areas like Maresme offer mild temperatures for riding.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Barcelones offers several easy routes. For a gentle ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995740917/ronda-verda-barcelona-seaside-trail-to-badalona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ronda Verda: Barcelona Seaside Trail to Badalona&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 21.3 km path along the coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983214193/paseo-maritimo-de-premia-de-mar-y-rio-besos-circular-route-of-barcelona-north" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo marítimo de Premia de Mar y río Besós - circular route of Barcelona north&lt;/a&gt; is another moderate option that follows coastal and river paths, suitable for those looking for a slightly longer but still accessible ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For advanced cyclists seeking challenging climbs, the natural parks surrounding Barcelona are ideal. Routes within &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937011165/from-the-turo-viewpoint-to-tibidabo-collserola-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collserola Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, such as the path leading to Tibidabo, offer substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983189012/collserola-park-and-rabassada-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collserola Park and Rabassada Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 77.7 km route with over 1500 meters of ascent, providing a demanding experience.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that offer scenic viewpoints or landmarks?
A: Absolutely. Many routes provide breathtaking views. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2687128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere Màrtir Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; within Collserola Natural Park offers panoramic vistas of Barcelona and the coastline. The iconic Montserrat Mountain, though a significant challenge, rewards cyclists with awe-inspiring 360-degree views of Catalonia. You can also find historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2500852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Baró Castle&lt;/a&gt; along some routes.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in the Barcelones area?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for endurance cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34563565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coco Beach – La Roca Road loop from Bac de Roda&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 100.4 km route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39542783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canet de Mar Beach – N-II road through Calella loop from Badalona Pompeu Fabra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 80 km, offering a good challenge for long-distance riders.

Q: Are there loop road cycling routes available?
A: Many road cycling routes in Barcelones are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983189012/collserola-park-and-rabassada-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collserola Park and Rabassada Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995624750/santa-creu-d-olorda-and-collserola-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Creu d'Olorda and Collserola Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995634654/creu-d-aragall-and-santa-creu-d-olorda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu d'Aragall and Santa Creu d'Olorda Loop&lt;/a&gt;, all offering circular journeys through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Barcelones?
A: Barcelones offers a highly varied terrain. You'll find flat coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea, gently rolling hills through the Penedès wine region, and demanding ascents into mountain ranges like Collserola, Montserrat, and Montseny. This diversity ensures options for every preference, from leisurely rides to intense mountain challenges.

Q: Are there road cycling routes accessible by public transport?
A: Barcelona's extensive public transport network, including trains and metro, can facilitate access to many starting points for road cycling routes. The city's prime location means many natural areas and challenging climbs are easily accessible from the city center, often within an hour's ride, making public transport a viable option for reaching your desired starting point.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with cultural or gastronomic experiences in Barcelones?
A: Absolutely. Barcelona's unique appeal lies in its ability to integrate urban exploration with natural adventures. You can cycle through areas near architectural marvels like Sagrada Familia or Parc Güell, or venture into the Penedès wine region for a more leisurely experience combining cycling with wine tourism and local cuisine. Many routes also pass through charming towns where you can find cafe stops.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Barcelones?
A: The road cycling routes in Barcelones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from stunning coastal views to challenging mountain climbs, and the ability to easily access natural parks from the city. The well-maintained roads and varied terrain are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

The ascent to the top of the Creu d'Aragall is a mountain pass that is located in the municipality of Gelida, and mainly occupies a mountainous area located at the northern end of the Ordal mountain range, connecting to the south with the Llobregat valley. .

   The vegetation in the mountainous area is typical of the Catalan pre-coastal mountains, mainly pine forest with undergrowth with bushes, being a very hot area in summer.

   The port from Gelida is 6.7 kilometers long, with an average slope of 6.2% and a maximum slope of 13% in the first stages.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Santa Creu d’Olorda circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a difficult road ride, requiring very good fitness. You should be prepared for demanding sections with continuous ascents and descents. Some climbs, like near Tibidabo, feature gradients exceeding 20%.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route typically takes nearly four hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and difficult for road bikes, potentially requiring you to push your bicycle.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy spectacular scenery with great panoramic views of Barcelona, the Mediterranean Sea, and the Llobregat delta. Notable spots include the Romanesque hermitage of Santa Creu d'Olorda, the Santa Creu d'Olorda Road, and the Creu d'Aragall Pass (537 m). The Collserola Natural Park itself offers a refreshing escape and stunning vistas.

faqs.question: Is this a circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a circular route, bringing you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the route is accessible from Barcelona, with climbs starting directly from the city, such as from Sarrià. Consider starting points with public transport access or designated parking within the Collserola Natural Park area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Collserola Natural Park is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Avoid the hottest summer days, especially given the route's demanding nature.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route through a natural park, dogs are generally permitted in the Collserola Natural Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure safety for all users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for the Collserola Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Collserola Natural Park for recreational activities like cycling. However, always respect park regulations and signage.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as El Camí Metropolità: de Montserrat a Barcelona, Bicivia 2, and Sant Feliu de Llobregat a Molins de Rei, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or a break along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Santa Creu d'Olorda area itself is a pleasant stopping point. It features a romantic chapel, a restaurant, and picnic areas, making it a good spot for a break.
